PublicChannel.description: The medium through which a service user interacts with the council (e.g., Phone, Online, In-Person).

CouncilAgent.description: Agents are interfaces which allow service users to contact and use a council’s services.

Capability.description: Capabilities are tools that council agents use to meet the needs of their service users.

ITSystem.description: The actual physical software application, SaaS, or platform (e.g., Generic CRM, GovPay).

FunctionGroup.description: The overarching service domain or directorate (e.g., Adult Social Care, Environmental Services).

nodes.label: In person
nodes.description: Some council services will still need to be delivered in-person. This includes at a council’s offices, at a household or business, or at another location.

nodes.label: Phone
nodes.description: Some services are delivered via telephone, allowing users to speak directly with council staff for support, enquiries, or transactions where digital access may not be suitable/available to the user.

nodes.label: Email
nodes.description: Asynchronous written communication via email.

nodes.label: Letter
nodes.description: Some services rely on postal communication, particularly for formal notifications, legal correspondence or where physical record of delivery is required.

nodes.label: Online
nodes.description: Many council services are delivered online through websites, portals or forms, enabling users to self-serve at a time that suits them.

nodes.label: SMS/ Messaging
nodes.description: Some services may use SMS or messaging channels to provide updates, reminders or quick interactions.

nodes.label: Social Media
nodes.description: Some councils may use social media platforms to share information, respond to enquiries and engage with residents in a more informal/accessible way.

nodes.label: Smart Device
nodes.description: Some services can be accessed through smart devices such as Internet of Things, to enable automated interaction where appropriate.

nodes.label: Third Party
nodes.description: Users may sometimes access council services through proxy; by going through their local MP or local media.

nodes.label: Video
nodes.description: Some councils may use video calls to deliver service remotely, allowing users to speak face-to-face with staff without needing to attend in person.

nodes.label: App
nodes.description: Native or cross-platform mobile application providing dedicated services to citizens.

nodes.label: API Gateway
nodes.description: Machine-to-machine interface handling third-party integrations and smart device inputs.

nodes.label: Automated Phone
nodes.description: Automated telephony system, such as IVR or conversational voicebots.

nodes.label: Human
nodes.description: Customer Service Representatives or specialist officers handling queries.

nodes.label: Public Device
nodes.description: These are devices owned by the council that are in the public domain, and include technology such as touch-screens in a public location for example in a library, or an internet of things device for example in a drain or bin.

nodes.label: Website
nodes.description: The primary council web portal, forms, and digital content interfaces.

nodes.label: Agentic AI
nodes.description: Autonomous AI agents capable of reasoning, executing multi-step tasks, and making routing decisions.

nodes.label: Booking
nodes.description: Councils use booking tools to arrange and manage appointments or service slots, such as inspections, and interviews with users.

nodes.label: Email
nodes.description: Emails are used as the primary tool to send and receive messages when dealing with users, internal/external stakeholders

nodes.label: Forms
nodes.description: Forms are used to collect and check information provided by users, such as in applications, reports and requests.

nodes.label: Identity
nodes.description: Councils use identity tools to check and verify who a user is

nodes.label: Payments
nodes.description: Payment tools are used to take and track payments made by users for services, such as council tax, licences or permits.

nodes.label: SMS
nodes.description: Councils use SMS tools to send and receive text messages for updates, reminders and quick communications with users.

nodes.label: Telephony / Fax
nodes.description: Councils use telephone (and in some cases where still needed, fax) to communicate/exchange information with their users.

nodes.label: Workflow
nodes.description: These are used to manage and track users request/application/casework as it moves through different processing stages.

nodes.label: Social Care System
nodes.description: Core case management system for Adult and Children's statutory social care.

nodes.label: Waste System
nodes.description: Operational system for managing domestic/commercial waste collection and fleet routing.

nodes.label: Electoral System
nodes.description: Specialist system for electoral registration and ballot management.

nodes.label: Corporate CRM
nodes.description: Centralised system for managing citizen interactions and service requests.

nodes.label: Revs and Bens System
nodes.description: System for processing council tax, business rates, and housing benefits.

nodes.label: Income Management System
nodes.description: Corporate income management, receipting, and cash management platform.

nodes.label: HR & Payroll System
nodes.description: Human resources, staff management, recruitment, and payroll processing.

nodes.label: Environmental Health System
nodes.description: Regulatory system for protection, trading standards, and food safety.

nodes.label: Housing Management System
nodes.description: System covering tenancies, allocations, rents, and repairs.

nodes.label: Leisure Management System
nodes.description: Booking and facility management platform for leisure centers and cultural venues.

nodes.label: Planning & Building Control System
nodes.description: System managing spatial planning applications and building control.

nodes.label: Highways Management System
nodes.description: Asset management system for roads, street lighting, and parking.

nodes.label: Corporate ERP
nodes.description: Enterprise Resource Planning for core back-office finance and procurement.

nodes.label: Library Management System
nodes.description: System for cataloging, loans, and membership management.

nodes.label: Education Management System
nodes.description: Management information system for schools, pupil admissions, and early years.
